name: domain-fusion-synthesis
description: Use web_search to search for 'fusion energy timeline commercial power grid 2030 2035 outlook realistic'. Results overlap with earlier fusion searches. Synthesize: when will fusion reach commercial viability? Key milestones ahead, main barriers, and realistic timeline. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Commercial fusion energy is most likely to reach the grid in the mid-2030s, with 35 of 45 companies surveyed by the IAEA targeting pilot plants between 2030 and 2035. Key near-term milestones include CFS achieving net energy from SPARC (~2027), Helion delivering 50 MW to Microsoft (~2028), and several companies beginning pilot plant construction. However, significant barriers remain: unproven materials that can survive intense neutron bombardment, insufficient global tritium supply, plasma stability at reactor scale, and projected costs well above current renewables. ITER's D-T operations are not expected until 2039. Realistically, while first-of-kind grid connections may occur by the mid-2030s, widespread commercial deployment contributing meaningfully to global electricity supply is more likely a 2040s-2050s prospect.
key_facts: 35 of 45 fusion companies surveyed target pilot plants between 2030 and 2035
First grid-connected fusion plants are most likely in the mid-2030s
Key barriers include materials durability, tritium supply, plasma stability, and high initial costs
Widespread commercial deployment is more realistically a 2040s-2050s prospect
ITER D-T operations not expected until 2039
